contract.requirement_text: Normative requirement statement derived from the canonical feature title.
contract.owner: Feature domain owner from the feature-map ledger.
contract.acceptance_criteria: Feature-map acceptance test or the default served-state evidence gate.
contract.status: Current served_state: served, conditional, partial, library, or roadmap.

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Certificate inventory.
items.acceptance_criteria: Vitest covers list/filter/empty/error/rate-limit states and an e2e screenshot proves certificate inventory renders from served API data.

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Network discovery.
items.acceptance_criteria: UI and API tests must prove a tenant can create a network source, queue a run, execute the served outbox worker, record a finding, and surface the discovered certificate without claiming unsupported source kinds as fully executed; CAP-NHI-03 served tests prove AD/cloud service-account discovery records metadata-only findings.
items.backend_status: served network source/run/finding control plane with outbox-executed TLS scan, certificate recording, and CAP-NHI-03 service-account findings

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Agent-based discovery.
items.acceptance_criteria: Served Go tests cover agent ReportInventory over mTLS producing discovery findings, graph nodes, discovery events, trust-store collector findings, and private-key-material findings without key bytes; Vitest covers agent list, enrollment-token creation, empty fleet, failed enrollment-token capability, and stale-agent warning states.
items.backend_status: served agent enrollment, mTLS heartbeat/renewal, and metadata-only filesystem, trust-store, and private-key-material inventory reports into discovery findings plus credential graph

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide CA-agnostic outbound issuance.
items.acceptance_criteria: E2E UI covers request-only principal denied from self-issue, approver approval, successful issue, idempotent replay, and error state.
items.backend_status: served for built-in path and configured external CA registry

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Built-in ACME server.
items.acceptance_criteria: UI shows ACME enabled/disabled, directory URL copy, last order status fixture, and fail-closed state when no issuing CA exists.

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Lifecycle automation.
items.acceptance_criteria: Served integration test proves ARI-window renewal queues ca.renew and records rotation evidence when the fixed renew_before threshold would not fire.
items.backend_status: manual transitions plus served ARI-first lifecycle renewal scheduler with outbox-driven ca.renew and rotation evidence

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Deployment connectors (initial set).
items.acceptance_criteria: Served acceptance creates a connector target, binds an identity, issues, deploys, rotates, rolls back, revokes, retires, and verifies connector delivery receipts; UI and CLI exercise target setup/actions.
items.backend_status: served native connector registry, signed-plugin outbox delivery, tenant target CRUD, identity binding, test/deploy/rollback actions, and receipt evidence

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide RBAC.
items.acceptance_criteria: UI tests permission-gated nav/actions and 403 messages that explain missing scope without leaking tenant data.

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Audit log surfaces.
items.acceptance_criteria: Vitest covers filters, export, empty/error states, event detail, and evidence bundle download status.

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide REST API.
items.acceptance_criteria: UI fetches OpenAPI, lists route groups, and tests unavailable/unauthenticated states without exposing tokens.

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide CLI.
items.acceptance_criteria: UI command snippets match internal/cli command table and redact bearer tokens.

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Web UI.
items.acceptance_criteria: A frontend parity test compares backlog items to registered GUI surfaces and fails if a served backend feature has no GUI mapping/disclosure.

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide SSO/OIDC (free).
items.acceptance_criteria: UI covers OIDC disabled, enabled redirect, /auth/me loaded, logout, and no fake-token login path.

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Single-binary distribution.
items.acceptance_criteria: UI displays build/runtime metadata from a served endpoint; if endpoint is absent, roadmap-disclosure card is shown.

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Encrypted control-plane transport.
items.acceptance_criteria: UI warns on dev/plaintext loopback and shows production TLS status without leaking private cert/key data.

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Crypto-agility and PQC readiness.
items.acceptance_criteria: UI maps CBOM/risk data into crypto-agility dashboard and tests weak/PQC-ready fixture states.

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Certificate Transparency monitoring.
items.acceptance_criteria: Served test proves a CT-log fixture certificate for a watched domain creates a ct_unexpected_issuance finding and dispatches an outbox-backed alert; UI keeps the dedicated CT dashboard gap visible.
items.backend_status: served ct_log discovery source/run/finding execution with outbox-backed unexpected-issuance alerts; dedicated CT dashboard/watchlist UI not served

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Drift detection.
items.acceptance_criteria: Served test proves a watched credential whose fingerprint drifts creates a credential_drift finding and the UI keeps remediation disabled until a served remediation workflow exists.
items.backend_status: served drift discovery source/run/finding execution with outbox-backed drift alerts; dedicated remediation dashboard not served

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Credential risk scoring.
items.acceptance_criteria: Vitest covers risk ranking, factor explanation, empty state, filtering, and navigation from risk row to graph/audit/certificate evidence.

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Plugin SDK with capability sandboxing.
items.acceptance_criteria: UI blocks unverified plugin activation and tests empty grants, denied capability, signature failure, and conformance failure states.
items.backend_status: served signed WASM CA and connector plugins with provenance, digest pins, capability grants, and reference plugin source trees

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Credential graph.
items.acceptance_criteria: Vitest covers node selection, blast-radius request, empty graph, error state, and URL-safe node IDs.

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide SSH credential discovery & inventory.
items.acceptance_criteria: Served Go tests prove private-key-material discovery reports metadata-only private_key findings over the agent channel; UI and API tests prove ssh discovery sources, schedules, runs, and findings can be represented without exposing key bytes or enabling trust mutation controls.
items.backend_status: served discovery control-plane source/run/finding records plus served agent private-key-material reports; SSH public-key scan execution remains agent/library-owned

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide ACME Renewal Information (ARI).
items.acceptance_criteria: Served lifecycle test proves a deployed short-lived certificate renews on its ARI window even when outside the fixed renew_before threshold.
items.backend_status: served via ACME renewal-info plus lifecycle scheduler consumption for trstctl-issued deployed X.509 identities

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide X.509 revocation infrastructure.
items.acceptance_criteria: UI covers revoke confirmation, reason selection, success, failure, OCSP/CRL unavailable, and audit link.

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Private/enterprise CA hierarchy management.
items.acceptance_criteria: Served test creates a root and intermediate through /api/v1/ca/*, enforces m-of-n ceremony approvals, and issues a leaf from the intermediate.

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Agentless cloud certificate discovery.
items.acceptance_criteria: Served Go tests prove a cloud_certificate run inventories ACM-compatible certificates through credential references, records discovery findings and certificate inventory, and never stores inline cloud credentials; UI/API tests prove source/run/finding rendering stays metadata-only.
items.backend_status: served discovery control-plane source/run/finding records plus outbox-executed AWS ACM, Azure Key Vault, and GCP Certificate Manager enumeration with credential references

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide EST server (RFC 7030).
items.acceptance_criteria: UI shows enabled/disabled states and curl/client examples; test verifies no page claims EST is active when config is off.

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide SCEP server (RFC 8894).
items.acceptance_criteria: UI covers GetCACaps/GetCACert/PKIOperation status and malformed request error evidence.

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide SPIFFE Workload API.
items.acceptance_criteria: UI shows disabled/misconfigured/enabled states and never exposes private key material from SVIDs.
items.backend_status: conditional served over UDS for X.509-SVID and JWT-SVID RPCs

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Ephemeral credential issuance.
items.acceptance_criteria: Served test opens an attestation-gated JIT request through /api/v1/ephemeral, enqueues approval outbox intent, records distinct approval, mints a short-TTL credential, and verifies expiry/idempotency.
items.backend_status: conditional served approval-gated ephemeral/JIT issuance endpoint

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide HSM integration.
items.acceptance_criteria: Served tests generate, rotate, revoke, and zeroize managed keys through the real API against LocalStack AWS KMS, Azure Key Vault HSM, GCP Cloud KMS, and a PKCS#11 HSM lifecycle backend; real AWS KMS runs opportunistically when AWS_* credentials are present. UI never exposes key material and displays only public metadata/health for configured backends.
items.backend_status: served AWS SDK v2-backed AWS KMS, Azure Key Vault HSM, GCP Cloud KMS, and PKCS#11 HSM managed-key custody via /api/v1/managed-keys; LocalStack acceptance covers AWS generate/rotate/revoke/zeroize; served CAP-KEY-02 acceptance covers Azure/GCP generate/rotate/revoke/zeroize; served CAP-KEY-01 acceptance covers PKCS#11 generate/rotate/revoke/zeroize; SoftHSM native binding remains cgo-proven

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Additional connectors.
items.acceptance_criteria: Served connector target CRUD and actions cover appliance connector names through the same outbox/native-registry/signed-plugin path; UI and CLI expose target setup, identity binding, deploy, and rollback.
items.backend_status: served native/signed-plugin outbox delivery for registered appliance connectors plus tenant target CRUD, identity binding, test/deploy/rollback actions, and receipt evidence

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Policy engine.
items.acceptance_criteria: UI covers allowed, denied, policy error, overload/fail-closed, and dry-run evidence states.
items.backend_status: served on mutation gate; authoring not full UI

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Notification integrations.
items.acceptance_criteria: Served lifecycle scheduler enqueues AlertBefore expiry notifications and dispatches a signed webhook through the notification outbox; failed dispatches are visible in the served dead-letter API and requeue idempotently.
items.backend_status: served expiry-alert runtime plus notification inbox/dead-letter API; channel authoring UI/API pending

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Workload attestation chain.
items.acceptance_criteria: UI renders attestation evidence without secret/token leakage and tests accepted, rejected, expired, wrong-tenant cases.
items.backend_status: conditional served attested X.509-SVID issuance endpoint with six-attester verifier

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Credential compromise workflow.
items.acceptance_criteria: Served API test executes a compromised identity remediation; CAP-ITDR-02 served test detects a compromised credential/stolen token from metadata-only evidence; UI tests render live execution and discovery evidence.

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Fleet re-issuance for CA compromise.
items.acceptance_criteria: UI must simulate staged plan with fixtures and block live run until served workflow exists.

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Just-in-time issuance with approval flows.
items.acceptance_criteria: UI covers requester cannot approve own request and approver can approve/deny with audit link.
items.backend_status: core approval gate, approval-gated JIT issuance, and PAM-lite Postgres/SSH sessions served

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Break-glass procedures.
items.acceptance_criteria: UI requires high-risk confirmation and shows library-only state until served; tests no one-click bypass.
items.backend_status: served-reconciliation; offline issuance remains operator ceremony

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide SSH certificate authority.
items.acceptance_criteria: Served acceptance covers SSH status, KRL version, revocation, CLI/UI handoff, disabled state, and no private-key exposure.
items.backend_status: conditional served CA, status, and KRL revocation workflow

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide SSH deployment & trust configuration (agent).
items.acceptance_criteria: Served acceptance records trust rollout with explicit confirmation, rollback/health evidence, and host retirement without browser-side host-file mutation.
items.backend_status: agent-side explicit opt-in with served rollout and retirement evidence API

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Attestation-gated short-lived SSH user certs.
items.acceptance_criteria: Served acceptance issues an attestation-gated SSH user cert through the API/CLI/UI path, emits attestation and SSH cert events, and never exposes private key material.
items.backend_status: conditional served attestation-gated SSH user cert API

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Code-signing service.
items.acceptance_criteria: Served test signs an artifact digest with a managed key and signs keylessly against Fulcio/Rekor fixtures through the real API, auth, idempotency, event log, and outbox dispatcher.

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Timestamping authority (RFC 3161).
items.acceptance_criteria: UI shows TSA enabled/disabled and verifies a fixture timestamp response status without exposing signing key material.

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Cryptographic discovery & observability (CBOM).
items.acceptance_criteria: internal/server/cbom_served_test.go drives POST /api/v1/cbom/scans over a real TLS endpoint and host config, then reads /api/v1/cbom/assets with FIPS 203/204/205 migration progress.

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Certificate profiles & registration-authority model.
items.acceptance_criteria: Vitest covers valid profile create, invalid JSON, server validation error, version display, and identity issuance using selected profile.

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Embedded / IoT enrollment agent.
items.acceptance_criteria: UI shows bootstrap available and renewal not mounted until served; tests cover token one-time visibility.

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide CMP server (RFC 4210 / CMPv3).
items.acceptance_criteria: UI covers enabled/disabled and served-error states with endpoint copy and docs link.

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Intune / MDM enrollment integration.
items.acceptance_criteria: UI makes MDM status conditional and tests challenge-required, challenge-missing, and disabled states.

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide PQC migration orchestration.
items.acceptance_criteria: internal/server/pqc_migration_served_test.go scans a CBOM RSA certificate-key estate, queues served ACME PQC re-issuance through the outbox, verifies 100% migration progress, and exercises rollback.

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide AI-agent / NHI identity broker.
items.acceptance_criteria: Served test requests a short-lived agent credential through /api/v1/broker/agent-identities, policy gates it, records it in the graph, and verifies the Workloads UI remains a disclosure-only broker surface.
items.backend_status: conditional served policy-gated AI/MCP agent identity broker endpoint

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Cryptographic compliance reporting & posture dashboards, plus NHI access certification campaigns.
items.acceptance_criteria: UI clearly says evidence is not certification; CAP-CMP-02 served tests prove WebTrust/ETSI evidence-pack posture is served; CAP-GOV-02 served tests prove NHI access-review campaigns start, decide, complete, and remain idempotent.
items.backend_status: served-api plus CAP-GOV-02 NHI access-review campaign workflow

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Secret store discovery.
items.acceptance_criteria: UI and API tests must prove secret_store discovery sources, runs, and findings never render, store, or submit secret values.
items.backend_status: served discovery control-plane source/run/finding records; external secret-store scan execution remains connector-owned

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide API key/token inventory.
items.acceptance_criteria: UI and API tests must prove api_key discovery sources, runs, and findings use masked references and never render token values.
items.backend_status: served discovery control-plane source/run/finding records plus access-admin API token metadata/list/create/revoke; external token scanner execution remains connector-owned

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Secret rotation engine.
items.acceptance_criteria: UI calls served rotate path for native store and hides backend-specific rotation controls until served.
items.backend_status: served native-store rotation plus rollback-safe static rotation API

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Ephemeral API key issuance.
items.acceptance_criteria: UI ensures token value is visible once only, cannot be recovered, and is never stored in localStorage/sessionStorage.

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Code/CI secret scanning bridge.
items.acceptance_criteria: UI proves finding snippets are redacted and actions require proper scope.

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Multi-tenant deployment topology.
items.acceptance_criteria: Execution tests prove CAP-MODEL-02 is served by provider-plane status plus idempotent managed tenant provisioning, and CAP-MODEL-04 is served by enterprise support/SLA/professional-services posture; UI tests cross-tenant denial, current tenant display, and no tenant ID taken from user-editable route fields.
items.backend_status: served RLS, tenancy, managed-provider tenant provisioning, and enterprise support/SLA posture

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Cross-cluster / multi-region federation.
items.acceptance_criteria: Integration test proves two clusters share trust and replicate read state within the documented RPO/RTO; UI test asserts passive federation copy instead of roadmap copy.

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Native secret store.
items.acceptance_criteria: Vitest proves secret values are not listed, reveal requires explicit user action, no browser storage of values, and disabled state when API/KEK absent.

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Developer secrets experience (CLI, portal, SDKs).
items.acceptance_criteria: UI examples match CLI/API contract, imports return metadata only, resolved reads are explicit, and generated snippets never include real secret values.
items.backend_status: served SDK path plus trstctl-cli run injection, reference resolution, and imports

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Dynamic secrets.
items.acceptance_criteria: Served test issues, renews, revokes, and expires a dynamic lease through the API; the leaseworker drains expiry revocations through the outbox-backed queue.

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Encryption-as-a-service (transit) & KMIP.
items.acceptance_criteria: UI never logs plaintext by default and marks test plaintext as local-only with explicit wiping behavior where possible.
items.backend_status: served transit API and CLI; served KMIP mTLS listener for AES-256 SymmetricKey Create/Get

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide PKI as a secrets engine.
items.acceptance_criteria: UI proves private key value is copy/download-once, no persistent browser storage, and disabled state when no issuing CA exists.

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Secret sync / platform integrations.
items.acceptance_criteria: Served test pushes a stored secret to GitHub Actions, AWS Secrets Manager, and Kubernetes fixtures through configured targets, verifies idempotent replay, and proves responses/events do not leak the secret value.
items.backend_status: conditional served for configured sync targets

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Platform auth-method framework.
items.acceptance_criteria: UI never displays credential secret after creation and tests tenant mismatch/fail-closed errors.
items.backend_status: served machine login for token, Kubernetes SAT, AWS IAM, GCP, Azure, OIDC, and generic JWT

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Non-human identity lifecycle management.
items.acceptance_criteria: Vitest covers multiple identity kinds, transition controls, status labels, audit links, and permission-gated actions.

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Secret sharing & secret-change approvals.
items.acceptance_criteria: UI proves share token is shown once, second redeem fails, and approval state is visible without value leakage.
items.backend_status: served durable one-time shares plus dual-control approvals for sensitive secret-store changes

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Pluggable AI model adapter.
items.acceptance_criteria: UI shows no-model/offline state by default and tests redaction/refusal warnings without showing secrets.

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Grounded RCA & natural-language query.
items.acceptance_criteria: UI covers grounded/cited RCA, insufficient evidence, disabled AI, and hostile record text rendered inert.

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide trstctl MCP server.
items.acceptance_criteria: UI proves tool list has no write tools, denied unknown tool state, rate-limit state, and cited result rendering.

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide DNS-01 challenge automation.
items.acceptance_criteria: UI uses secret references, never raw provider token persistence in browser, and tests provider-error states.

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide DNS-provider plugin framework.
items.acceptance_criteria: UI distinguishes built-in providers from plugin providers and blocks activation without verified conformance/provenance.

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide CNAME delegation for validation isolation.
items.acceptance_criteria: UI explains production DNS isolation and tests passing/failing delegation fixtures.

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide CAA policy enforcement & management.
items.acceptance_criteria: UI covers no CAA, allowed, denied, DNS failure, and wildcard cases.

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Multi-method domain-validation policy.
items.acceptance_criteria: UI covers method selection preview and policy-denied challenge states.

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Automated wildcard issuance & renewal.
items.acceptance_criteria: UI requires explicit acknowledgement for wildcard profile/issuance and shows DNS-01-only constraint.

items.requirement_text: trstctl shall provide Unified semantic query layer.
items.acceptance_criteria: UI covers grounded answer, insufficient evidence, disabled AI, citation navigation, and injection-inert record text.
